title: Fix design-ready toggle and admin login/authentication on live app

requirements.summary: Fix the 'Is your design ready?' option selector on QuotationRequestPage so clicking Yes/No options visually updates the selected state and correctly stores the chosen value
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Yes' highlights the Yes option and deselects No
Clicking 'No' highlights the No option and deselects Yes
The selected value is correctly passed when the quotation form is submitted
The toggle state is visually distinct (e.g., filled/colored vs. outline) for selected vs. unselected

file_operations.description: Fix the design status toggle by ensuring the designStatus state is properly updated when a user clicks Yes or No. Update the button click handlers to set designStatus to 'ready' or 'needed' respectively. Ensure the visual styling (background color, border, text color) reflects the active selection by comparing each button's value against the current designStatus state. Add proper Tailwind classes for selected (filled background with primary color) vs. unselected (outline/border only) states.

requirements.summary: Fix admin authentication and routing logic so valid admin login via email/password correctly redirects to Admin Dashboard instead of showing setup or home prompts
acceptanceCriteria: Entering valid admin email and password on the live app redirects to /admin/dashboard
AdminGuard correctly reads the session and grants access to admin routes after login
The 'Go to Admin Setup' page is NOT shown to a user who has successfully authenticated with valid admin credentials
If no admin exists yet, the setup page is shown; if admin exists and credentials are valid, the dashboard is shown
Admin login via email/password works on the deployed/live app URL, not just in draft mode

file_operations.description: Review and fix the email/password authentication flow to ensure that after successful backend verification, both the admin email and a verified session flag are stored consistently in sessionStorage (e.g., 'adminEmail' and 'adminAuthenticated'). After setting sessionStorage, invalidate all React Query caches using queryClient.invalidateQueries() to ensure AdminGuard and useAdminExists hooks re-fetch. Add explicit navigation to '/admin/dashboard' using navigate() after successful authentication. Ensure the authentication mutation's onSuccess handler waits for cache invalidation to complete before navigating.

Fix the AdminGuard logic to correctly detect authenticated admin sessions by checking both sessionStorage keys ('adminEmail' and 'adminAuthenticated') and Internet Identity principal. Ensure the guard does not redirect an authenticated email/password admin to the setup page. If sessionStorage contains 'adminAuthenticated' === 'true' and a valid 'adminEmail', treat the user as authenticated and skip the admin-exists check. Only redirect to setup if no admin exists AND the user is not already authenticated via email/password. Ensure the guard reads sessionStorage values synchronously before making routing decisions.

requirements.summary: Add a clearly visible, standalone 'Admin Login' link in the footer so admins can navigate directly to /admin/login
acceptanceCriteria: A link or button labeled 'Admin Login' is visible on the live site (e.g., in the footer)
Clicking it navigates to the /admin/login route
The link is unobtrusive and does not disrupt the customer-facing design

file_operations.description: Add a small, discreet 'Admin Login' link in the footer section below the existing contact info and social links. Use a muted text color (text-gray-500 or similar) and small font size to keep it unobtrusive. Wire the link to navigate to '/admin/login' using TanStack Router's Link component. Position it at the bottom-right or center of the footer to avoid disrupting the main customer-facing content.
